$description: Data-driven repo archetype detection registry for aiframework. Each archetype defines detection signals and CLAUDE.md customization rules.

library.description: Reusable library or package published to a registry

heuristics: Has lib/ or src/ but no app/ or pages/

Publishing.description: How to publish a new version
Publishing.template:
```bash
# Bump version
# Run tests
# Publish to registry
```

API Stability.description: Semantic versioning policy and breaking change process
API Stability.template: This library follows semver. Breaking changes require a MAJOR version bump and migration guide.

extra_invariants.rule: All public API changes must follow semantic versioning
All public functions/types must have documentation with examples
No runtime dependencies on specific frameworks — keep the library generic

Installation.description: How to install the CLI tool
Installation.template:
```bash
# Install from source
# Install from registry
```

extra_invariants.rule: All commands must have --help output with usage examples
Exit codes must be meaningful — 0 for success, non-zero for specific error categories
All user-facing output must go to stdout, errors/warnings to stderr

heuristics: Has pages/ or app/ directory with UI components
Has public/ or static/ directory with assets

Local Development.description: How to run the app locally
Local Development.template:
```bash
# Install dependencies
# Start dev server
# Access at http://localhost:PORT
```

Browser Support.template: Modern evergreen browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

extra_invariants.rule: All pages must be accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum)
No secrets in client-side bundles — all sensitive config via server-side env vars
All forms must have client-side and server-side validation

heuristics: Has web framework in dependencies but no frontend components

API Documentation.description: Where to find API docs and how to generate them
API Documentation.template: API documentation is available at `/docs` (Swagger) or `/redoc`.

Authentication.template: Bearer token in Authorization header. See auth docs for token generation.

extra_invariants.rule: All endpoints must be documented in OpenAPI/GraphQL schema
All endpoints must have request/response validation
Health check endpoint must exist at /health or /healthz

full-stack.description: Application with both frontend and backend components

heuristics: Has both frontend/ and backend/ directories
Has both client/ and server/ directories
Has both UI components and API routes in the same repo

Architecture.description: How frontend and backend components interact
Architecture.template: Frontend communicates with backend via REST/GraphQL API. See architecture docs for data flow.

Local Development.description: How to run the full stack locally
Local Development.template:
```bash
# Start backend
# Start frontend
# Or use docker-compose for everything
```

extra_invariants.rule: Frontend and backend must be independently deployable
API contracts between frontend and backend must be typed (OpenAPI, GraphQL schema, tRPC)

heuristics: Has packages/ or apps/ with multiple package.json files
Has workspace configuration (lerna, pnpm, turborepo, nx)

Workspace Structure.description: Map of packages/apps and their purposes

Cross-Package Development.template: Use workspace links for local development. Run `<tool> build` from root for full rebuild.

extra_invariants.rule: Shared dependencies must be hoisted to root — no duplicate versions across packages
Each package must have its own test suite — no cross-package test dependencies
Package boundaries must be respected — import only through public API

data-pipeline.description: ETL, data processing, or data engineering project

heuristics: Heavy use of pandas, spark, or SQL files

Pipeline Architecture.description: Data flow from source to destination

Data Sources.description: External data sources and their refresh schedules

extra_invariants.rule: All pipelines must be idempotent — safe to re-run without duplicating data
All data transformations must have data quality checks (row counts, null checks, schema validation)
PII data must be masked or encrypted in non-production environments

ml-project.description: ML model training, evaluation, and serving

heuristics: Has training scripts with model definitions
Heavy use of numpy, pandas, torch, tensorflow

Model Registry.description: Where models are stored and versioned
Model Registry.template: Models are tracked in MLflow/W&B. Production models are stored in <registry>.

Experiment Tracking.description: How to track and compare experiments
Experiment Tracking.template: Use MLflow/W&B for experiment tracking. All experiments must log metrics, parameters, and artifacts.

Data Requirements.description: Training data sources and preprocessing steps
Data Requirements.template: See data/ directory for dataset documentation and preprocessing scripts.

extra_invariants.rule: All experiments must be tracked with reproducible parameters and metrics
Model artifacts must be versioned — no overwriting production models
Training data must never leak into test/validation sets

mobile-app.description: iOS, Android, or cross-platform mobile app

heuristics: Has React Native or Flutter configuration

Build & Release.description: How to build and release the app
Build & Release.template:
```bash
# iOS build
# Android build
# Release to stores
```

Device Testing.description: How to test on devices and simulators
Device Testing.template: Test on physical devices before release. Minimum OS versions: iOS 15+, Android 10+.

extra_invariants.rule: No API keys or secrets in client-side code — use server-side token exchange
All network calls must handle offline state gracefully
Sensitive data must use platform secure storage (Keychain/Keystore) — not SharedPreferences/UserDefaults

infrastructure.description: Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, or platform engineering

heuristics: Has Helm charts or Kubernetes manifests

Environment Matrix.description: Mapping of environments and their configurations
Environment Matrix.template:
| Environment | Purpose | Access | Region |
|------------|---------|--------|--------|
| dev | Development | Team | us-east-1 |
| staging | Pre-production | Team | us-east-1 |
| prod | Production | Restricted | us-east-1, eu-west-1 |

Runbook.template: See docs/runbook/ for incident response and operational procedures.

extra_invariants.rule: All infrastructure changes must go through plan/review before apply — no manual changes
All secrets must be managed through a secrets manager — no plaintext secrets in IaC
All resources must be tagged with owner, environment, and cost-center

documentation-site.description: Documentation, knowledge base, or static content site

heuristics: Has content/ or docs/ as primary directory

Content Guidelines.description: How to write and organize documentation
Content Guidelines.template: Follow the style guide in docs/STYLE_GUIDE.md. Use MDX for interactive components.

Navigation.template: Navigation is auto-generated from directory structure. Override in sidebar config.

extra_invariants.rule: All internal links must be validated — no broken links
All code examples must be tested or verified — no stale snippets

detection_notes: Archetypes are checked in order. First match wins, but monorepo can combine with other archetypes (e.g., monorepo + web-app). The detection_order prioritizes more specific archetypes before generic ones.
